summary: BGMI Tournament Website (Raj Empire Esports) with player registration, mobile OTP login, BGMI Player ID verification, UPI/QR payment system with screenshot upload, admin approval system for room ID/password unlock, tournament management with auto-close when full, per-tournament match rules display, an admin-editable Terms & Conditions page shown on registration/payment with agree checkbox, a Support Ticket System where players can submit tickets with screenshots and admins can reply and manage ticket status, and an admin-editable Social Links section (YouTube, Instagram, Telegram) displayed in the site footer.
architectural_notes: Mobile OTP authentication used instead of password-based login
One account per mobile number to prevent duplicate registrations
Room ID/Password locked until admin manually approves payment screenshot
Admin credentials hardcoded: username='Empire Esports', password='Shivam803119&'
Default UPI ID for payments: empirerajesports@ibl
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Bold tactical lettering logo spelling 'Raj Empire Esports' with orange and gold gradient text on a dark/transparent background, military-style font (raj-empire-esports-logo.dim_400x120.png)
- Stylized dark-themed QR code graphic with orange border accents and 'Scan to Pay' text centered below the QR pattern on a near-black background (upi-qr-placeholder.dim_300x300.png)

existing_features.title: Implement a player registration and login system where each player registers with their mobile number (OTP-based verification simulated via a 4-digit code), a unique BGMI Player ID (mandatory), and a display name. Enforce one account per mobile number — if a mobile number already exists, block new registration and show an error.

Build a tournament listing page where players can view all active tournaments. Each tournament card shows: tournament name, entry fee, prize pool, total slots, filled slots, and registration status. Players must be logged in to register for a tournament.

Implement a payment flow where after a player registers for a tournament, they are shown a UPI ID and a QR code image for payment. The player must upload a screenshot of the payment proof (image file). The submission is saved with status 'Pending Admin Approval'. Room ID and Password remain hidden until admin approves the payment.

Build an admin dashboard (accessible via a separate admin login with hardcoded credentials) that allows the admin to: view all tournament registrations with payment screenshots, approve or reject each payment, add/edit/delete tournaments (name, date/time, entry fee, prize pool, map, total slots, UPI ID, QR code, Room ID, Room Password), and view the full player list.

After admin approves a player's payment for a tournament, display the Room ID and Room Password to that player on their dashboard and on the tournament detail page. Room details must only be visible to players whose payments are approved. Late join is not allowed — once a tournament's slot count is full, auto-close registration and mark it as 'Full'.

Design the entire website with a dark, high-energy gaming aesthetic inspired by battle royale games. Use a deep dark background (near-black), bold accent colors (orange and gold), sharp angular UI elements, and military/tactical typography. Apply this theme consistently across all pages: login, player dashboard, tournament list, tournament detail, payment flow, and admin panel.

Generate a BGMI Arena logo image (bgmi-arena-logo.dim_400x120.png) featuring bold tactical lettering with orange and gold colors on a dark/transparent background, suitable for use as a site header logo.

Generate a UPI QR code placeholder image (upi-qr-placeholder.dim_300x300.png) showing a stylized dark-themed QR code graphic with 'Scan to Pay' text, used in the payment flow as the default QR display when no QR is uploaded by admin.
Replace all occurrences of the site name (e.g., 'BGMI Arena', or any previous branding) with 'Raj Empire Esports' across every page, component, tab title, header, and footer in the frontend.

Generate a new site header logo image (raj-empire-esports-logo.dim_400x120.png) featuring bold tactical lettering spelling 'Raj Empire Esports' with orange and gold colors on a dark/transparent background, replacing the previous logo.

In the admin dashboard's tournament add/edit form, add a 'Match Rules' textarea input so the admin can write per-tournament rules. The saved rules must persist and be returned when fetching tournament details.

On the tournament detail/listing page and the custom room join section, display the Match Rules below each tournament card/room detail. Only show the rules section if matchRules text is non-empty. Style it with the dark gaming theme (orange/gold accents, military typography).

Add a dedicated Terms & Conditions page at route '/terms' that displays the current T&C content fetched from the backend. Style the page with the dark gaming theme consistent with Raj Empire Esports branding.

In the admin dashboard, add a 'Terms & Conditions' management section where the admin can view and edit the T&C content in a rich textarea. Save changes via the backend update function. Style consistently with the admin panel dark theme.

Add a 'Support' page/section in the player dashboard where the logged-in player can: (1) create a new support ticket by filling in subject, description, and optionally uploading a screenshot; (2) view a list of their submitted tickets showing subject, status badge (Open/Replied/Closed), and creation date; (3) click a ticket to view its full detail including description, uploaded screenshot (if any), and admin reply (if any). Style all UI with the dark gaming theme (orange/gold accents, angular elements) consistent with Raj Empire Esports branding.

Add a 'Support Tickets' section in the admin dashboard where the admin can: (1) view all player-submitted support tickets in a list showing player name, BGMI Player ID, subject, status, and creation date; (2) click a ticket to open its detail view with the player's description and screenshot (if any); (3) type and submit a reply to any ticket; (4) update the ticket status (Open / Replied / Closed) via a dropdown or buttons. Style consistently with the existing admin panel dark gaming theme.

Build the player registration page with fields: mobile number, display name, BGMI Player ID (mandatory). Simulate OTP: after entering mobile, show a 4-digit OTP input step (simulate by displaying the code or having backend generate it). Enforce one account per mobile — show a clear error if mobile already exists. Include an 'I agree to the Terms & Conditions' checkbox with a clickable link to /terms; disable the submit button until checked.
Build the player login page using mobile number + OTP (no password). Player enters mobile, receives simulated OTP, enters it to log in. On success, redirect to player dashboard.

Build the tournament listing page showing all active tournaments as cards. Each card displays: tournament name, entry fee, prize pool, total slots, filled slots, map, date/time, and registration status (Open/Full). If matchRules is non-empty, display it below the card. Players must be logged in to register. When a tournament's slots are full, show it as 'Full' and disable registration.

Implement the payment flow: after a player clicks Register on a tournament, show the tournament's UPI ID and QR code image (fallback to upi-qr-placeholder.dim_300x300.png if no QR uploaded). Player uploads a payment screenshot. Include 'I agree to the Terms & Conditions' checkbox. On submission, save registration with status 'Pending Admin Approval'. Room ID and Password remain hidden until admin approves.

Build the player dashboard showing: player's display name, BGMI Player ID, their tournament registrations with status badges (Pending/Approved/Rejected). For Approved registrations, display Room ID and Room Password. Include a Support section where the player can create new support tickets (subject, description, optional screenshot upload), view their ticket list with status badges (Open/Replied/Closed), and click a ticket to see full details including admin reply.

Build the admin dashboard accessible via a separate login page with hardcoded credentials. Admin dashboard sections: (1) Tournament Management — add/edit/delete tournaments with all fields including matchRules textarea, UPI ID, QR code upload, Room ID, Room Password; (2) Registrations — list all registrations with player info and payment screenshots, approve or reject each; (3) Player List — view all registered players; (4) Terms & Conditions — view and edit T&C content in a textarea with save; (5) Support Tickets — list all tickets with player name, BGMI ID, subject, status, date; click to view detail with screenshot; reply to ticket; update ticket status via dropdown/buttons.

Add a dedicated Terms & Conditions page at route /terms that fetches and displays the current T&C content from the backend. Style with the dark gaming theme consistent with Raj Empire Esports branding.

Apply a dark, high-energy battle-royale gaming aesthetic consistently across all pages. Use near-black deep dark background, bold orange and gold accent colors, sharp angular UI elements, and military/tactical typography. Brand all pages, tab title, header, and footer as 'Raj Empire Esports'. Use raj-empire-esports-logo.dim_400x120.png as the site header logo.

Build the player registration page with fields: mobile number, display name, BGMI Player ID (mandatory). After entering mobile, show a 4-digit OTP input step (display the simulated OTP on screen). Enforce one account per mobile — show a clear error if mobile already exists. Include an 'I agree to the Terms & Conditions' checkbox with a clickable link to /terms; disable the submit button until checked.
Build the player login page using mobile number + OTP (no password). Player enters mobile, receives simulated OTP displayed on screen, enters it to log in. On success, redirect to player dashboard.

Build the tournament listing page showing all active tournaments as cards. Each card displays: tournament name, entry fee, prize pool, total slots, filled slots, map, date/time, and registration status (Open/Full). If matchRules is non-empty, display it below the card. Players must be logged in to register. When a tournament's slots are full, show it as 'Full' and disable the Register button.

Implement the payment flow: after a player clicks Register on a tournament, show the tournament's UPI ID (default: empirerajesports@ibl) and QR code image (fallback to upi-qr-placeholder.dim_300x300.png if no QR uploaded). Player uploads a payment screenshot. Include an 'I agree to the Terms & Conditions' checkbox. On submission, save registration with status 'Pending Admin Approval'. Room ID and Password remain hidden until admin approves.

Build the admin dashboard accessible via a separate login page with hardcoded credentials (username: 'Empire Esports', password: 'Shivam803119&'). Admin dashboard must have five sections: (1) Tournament Management — add/edit/delete tournaments with all fields including matchRules textarea, UPI ID, QR code upload, Room ID, Room Password; (2) Registrations — list all registrations with player info and payment screenshots, approve or reject each; (3) Player List — view all registered players; (4) Terms & Conditions — view and edit T&C content in a textarea with a save button; (5) Support Tickets — list all tickets with player name, BGMI ID, subject, status, date; click to view detail with screenshot; reply to ticket; update ticket status via dropdown/buttons.

Build the player registration page at route /register with fields: mobile number, display name, BGMI Player ID (mandatory). After entering mobile, show a 4-digit OTP input step and display the simulated OTP on screen. Enforce one account per mobile — show a clear error if mobile already exists. Include an 'I agree to the Terms & Conditions' checkbox with a clickable link to /terms; disable the submit button until the checkbox is checked. On success, redirect to the player dashboard.

Build the player login page at route /login using mobile number + OTP (no password). Player enters mobile, receives simulated OTP displayed on screen, enters it to log in. On success, redirect to the player dashboard.

Build the tournament listing page at route /tournaments showing all active tournaments as cards. Each card displays: tournament name, entry fee, prize pool, total slots, filled slots, map, date/time, and registration status (Open/Full). If matchRules is non-empty, display it below the card. Players must be logged in to register. When a tournament's slots are full, show it as 'Full' and disable the Register button.

Implement the payment flow: after a player clicks Register on a tournament, show a payment modal/page with the tournament's UPI ID (default: empirerajesports@ibl) and QR code image (fallback to upi-qr-placeholder.dim_300x300.png if no QR uploaded by admin). Player uploads a payment screenshot (image file). Include an 'I agree to the Terms & Conditions' checkbox. On submission, save registration with status 'Pending Admin Approval'. Room ID and Password remain hidden until admin approves.

Build the player dashboard at route /dashboard showing: player's display name and BGMI Player ID, their tournament registrations with status badges (Pending/Approved/Rejected). For Approved registrations, display Room ID and Room Password. Include a Support section where the player can create new support tickets (subject, description, optional screenshot upload), view their ticket list with status badges (Open/Replied/Closed), and click a ticket to see full details including admin reply.

Build the admin dashboard accessible via a separate login page at route /admin with hardcoded credentials (username: 'Empire Esports', password: 'Shivam803119&'). Admin dashboard must have six sections: (1) Tournament Management — add/edit/delete tournaments with all fields including matchRules textarea, UPI ID, QR code upload, Room ID, Room Password; (2) Registrations — list all registrations with player info and payment screenshots, approve or reject each; (3) Player List — view all registered players; (4) Terms & Conditions — view and edit T&C content in a textarea with a save button; (5) Support Tickets — list all tickets with player name, BGMI ID, subject, status, date; click to view detail with screenshot; reply to ticket; update ticket status via dropdown/buttons; (6) Social Links — input fields for YouTube, Instagram, and Telegram URLs with a save button.

Apply a dark, high-energy battle-royale gaming aesthetic consistently across all pages. Use a near-black deep dark background, bold orange and gold accent colors, sharp angular UI elements, and military/tactical typography. Brand all pages, tab title, header, and footer as 'Raj Empire Esports'. Use raj-empire-esports-logo.dim_400x120.png as the site header logo. Display the Social Links (YouTube, Instagram, Telegram) fetched from the backend in the site footer as icon links.

Build the player registration page at route /register with fields: mobile number, display name, BGMI Player ID (mandatory). After entering mobile, show a 4-digit OTP input step and display the simulated OTP on screen. Enforce one account per mobile — show a clear error if mobile already exists. Include an 'I agree to the Terms & Conditions' checkbox with a clickable link to /terms; disable the submit button until the checkbox is checked. On success, redirect to the player dashboard at /dashboard.

Build the player login page at route /login using mobile number + OTP (no password). Player enters mobile, receives simulated OTP displayed on screen, enters it to log in. On success, redirect to the player dashboard at /dashboard.

new_feature_requests.summary: BGMI Player ID mandatory field, one account per mobile number
Manual UPI/QR payment system with screenshot upload
Admin approval system to verify payment and unlock Room ID/Password
Room ID/Password shown only after payment is approved by admin
Auto-close tournament room when player limit is full
Admin dashboard for tournament and player management
Per-tournament Match Rules section shown below each custom room/tournament card, editable by admin when creating/editing tournaments
Admin-editable Terms & Conditions page shown on registration/payment page with 'I agree to T&C' checkbox

Add a 'Match Rules' field to the tournament data model in the backend. When admin creates or edits a tournament, they must be able to enter match rules (multi-line text). Store this field alongside other tournament data.
Add a Terms & Conditions data model in the backend with a single editable text field (content). Provide backend functions to: get the current T&C content, and update the T&C content (admin only).

On the player registration page and the payment submission page, add a 'I agree to the Terms & Conditions' checkbox with a clickable link to open the T&C (either in a modal or navigating to /terms). The form must not be submittable unless the checkbox is checked.

Support Ticket System: Players can create tickets (subject + description + optional screenshot), view their tickets and status (Open/Replied/Closed), and see admin replies. Admin dashboard has a Support Tickets section to view all tickets, reply, and update status.

Add a Support Ticket data model in the backend with fields: ticketId, playerId, playerName, subject, description, screenshotBlob (optional), status (Open/Replied/Closed), createdAt, adminReply (optional), repliedAt (optional). Provide backend functions: createTicket (player creates a ticket with subject, description, optional screenshot), getMyTickets (player fetches their own tickets), getAllTickets (admin fetches all tickets), replyToTicket (admin replies to a ticket and sets status to Replied), updateTicketStatus (admin sets status to Open/Replied/Closed).

Implement the complete backend in a single Motoko actor with all data models and functions: Player (mobile, bgmiPlayerId, displayName, OTP simulation), Tournament (name, dateTime, entryFee, prizePool, map, totalSlots, filledSlots, upiId, qrCodeBlob, roomId, roomPassword, matchRules, status), TournamentRegistration (registrationId, tournamentId, playerId, paymentScreenshotBlob, status: Pending/Approved/Rejected), TermsAndConditions (content), and SupportTicket (ticketId, playerId, playerName, subject, description, screenshotBlob, status: Open/Replied/Closed, createdAt, adminReply, repliedAt). Expose all CRUD, query, and mutation functions needed by the frontend.

Implement the complete Motoko backend in a single actor with all data models and functions: Player (mobile, bgmiPlayerId, displayName, OTP simulation), Tournament (name, dateTime, entryFee, prizePool, map, totalSlots, filledSlots, upiId, qrCodeBlob, roomId, roomPassword, matchRules, status), TournamentRegistration (registrationId, tournamentId, playerId, paymentScreenshotBlob, status: Pending/Approved/Rejected), TermsAndConditions (content), and SupportTicket (ticketId, playerId, playerName, subject, description, screenshotBlob optional, status: Open/Replied/Closed, createdAt, adminReply optional, repliedAt optional). Expose all CRUD, query, and mutation functions needed by the frontend including OTP generation/verification, tournament slot management (auto-close when full), admin approval of registrations, and support ticket reply/status update.
Admin-editable Social Links section (YouTube, Instagram, Telegram links) — admin can add/edit links from dashboard, links displayed in site footer for players

Implement the complete Motoko backend in a single actor with all data models and functions: Player (mobile, bgmiPlayerId, displayName, OTP simulation), Tournament (name, dateTime, entryFee, prizePool, map, totalSlots, filledSlots, upiId, qrCodeBlob, roomId, roomPassword, matchRules, status), TournamentRegistration (registrationId, tournamentId, playerId, paymentScreenshotBlob, status: Pending/Approved/Rejected), TermsAndConditions (content), SupportTicket (ticketId, playerId, playerName, subject, description, screenshotBlob optional, status: Open/Replied/Closed, createdAt, adminReply optional, repliedAt optional), and SocialLinks (youtube, instagram, telegram). Expose all CRUD, query, and mutation functions: OTP generation/verification, tournament slot management (auto-close when filledSlots reaches totalSlots), admin approval of registrations (unlocks roomId/roomPassword for that player), support ticket create/reply/status-update, T&C get/update, and social links get/update.

Implement the complete Motoko backend in a single actor with all data models and functions: Player (mobile, bgmiPlayerId, displayName, OTP simulation), Tournament (name, dateTime, entryFee, prizePool, map, totalSlots, filledSlots, upiId, qrCodeBlob, roomId, roomPassword, matchRules, status), TournamentRegistration (registrationId, tournamentId, playerId, paymentScreenshotBlob, status: Pending/Approved/Rejected), TermsAndConditions (content), SupportTicket (ticketId, playerId, playerName, subject, description, screenshotBlob optional, status: Open/Replied/Closed, createdAt, adminReply optional, repliedAt optional), and SocialLinks (youtube, instagram, telegram). Expose all CRUD, query, and mutation functions: OTP generation/verification, tournament slot management (auto-close when filledSlots reaches totalSlots), admin approval of registrations (unlocks roomId/roomPassword for approved player), support ticket create/reply/status-update, T&C get/update, and social links get/update.
